questions.question: Which of the following protocols is commonly used for data encapsulation in WANs ?
questions.explanation: HDLC (High-Level Data Link Control) is a group of data link layer communication protocols that transmit data between network nodes.

questions.question: What does QR in 'QR code' stand for?
questions.explanation: Quick Response. A QR code is a type of barcode that can be read easily by a digital device and which stores information as a series of pixels in a square-shaped grid.

questions.question: Which browser extension could be used to download an entire web page for offline use?
questions.explanation: Web ScrapBook. It captures web pages to local devices or backend servers for future retrieval, organization, annotation, and editing. Q.1674. Which of the following is considered the first mechanical computer designed by Charles Babbage? SSC CHSL Tier II 18/11/2024 (a) ENIAC (b) Analytical Engine (c) Abacus (d) UNIVAC Sol.1674.(b) Analytical Engine. It was proposed in 1837. It featured an Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU), programmatic flow control (like loops and conditional branching), and used punched cards for programmability.

questions.question: Which of the following keyboard shortcuts is commonly used to rename a file or folder in Windows OS?
questions.explanation: F2. A standard PC keyboard has 12 function keys, labeled F1 through F12, in the top row of the keyboard.

questions.question: Keyboard shortcut to Save as document in MS Word 2019 is:
questions.explanation: F12. Shortcut Keys in MS Word 2019: F1: Opens Help, F2: Moves text or objects (similar to Cut), F3: Inserts the AutoText entry, F4: Repeats the last action, F5: Opens the Find and Replace dialog box with the Go To tab active. Q.1677. What application software is utilised for managing extensive data collections and executing queries? SSC CHSL Tier II 18/11/2024 (a) Antivirus software (b) Database management system software (c) Word processing software (d) Paint Sol.1677.(b) Popular DBMS Software: Relational DBMS (RDBMS) - Oracle Database, MySQL, PostgreSQL, Microsoft SQL Server. NoSQL DBMS (Non-Relational Databases) - MongoDB, Cassandra, Redis. Cloud-Based DBMS - Amazon RDS, Google Cloud Firestore, Azure SQL Database.

questions.question: In symmetric encryption, how many keys are used to encrypt and decrypt data?
questions.explanation: One Key. Symmetric encryption (or “private key” encryption) is called “private key” because the use of a single encryption key necessitates that the key is always kept private.

questions.question: What is the function of the CPU after the DMA transfer starts?

options.a: As long as the transfer is handled by the DMA controller, it is free to perform other tasks.
options.b: It plays a role in the entire data transfer.
options.c: It remains inactive until the DMA transfer is completed.
options.d: It actively manages each byte transferred.

questions.explanation: Direct Memory Access (DMA) is a technique that allows data to be transferred between memory and input/output devices without involving the processor.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the primary difference between broadband and dial-up Internet connections?

options.a: Dial-up requires a cable connection, while broadband does not.
options.b: Broadband is only available in urban areas, while dial-up is available everywhere.
options.c: Dial-up uses a telephone line, while broadband uses various technologies for higher speeds.

questions.explanation: Dial-up: Uses a traditional telephone line to connect to the internet. This method is slow and can be interrupted by phone calls. Broadband: Uses various technologies like cable, fiber optic, or DSL to provide high-speed internet access. It offers significantly faster speeds than dial-up and is not interrupted by phone calls. Examples of Internet Service Providers (Broadband): Jio, Airtel, Vi, BSNL, Atria Convergence Technologies, Hathway, Exitel Broadband.

questions.question: Average function is listed in the drop - down menu of which of the following tools?
questions.explanation: Auto Sum function. The Average function is typically listed in the AutoSum function drop-down menu in spreadsheet software like Microsoft Excel. The AutoSum function provides a quick way to calculate common functions like Sum, Average, Count, Max, and Min. Q.1682. Which of the following are needed for Skype to function on a computer? 1) A web cam 2) A microphone 3) A speaker SSC CHSL 01/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) Only 1 and 2 (b) Only 2 and 3 (c) 1, 2 and 3 (d) Only 1 and 3 Sol.1682.(c) 1, 2 and 3. Skype is a communication platform that allows users to make voice and video calls, send instant messages, and share files over the internet.

questions.question: The default width of a column in MS-Excel is:
questions.explanation: 8 characters. In Microsoft Excel, the default width of a column is set to accommodate 8.43 characters, which is approximately 64 pixels. Here are some of the default settings in Microsoft Excel: Row Height: 15 points (or about 20 pixels). Font: Calibri, Font Size: 11. Cell Alignment: Horizontal Alignment- General , Vertical Alignment- Bottom. Cell Fill Color: No fill (white cm) on all sides. Zoom Level: 100%.

questions.question: In MS Word, which option allows you to align text in a way that it appears vertically at the bottom of a cell or text box?
questions.explanation: Bottom Alignment. This option ensures that the text is positioned at the bottom edge, leaving space above it. Center Alignment: Centers text horizontally in the cell or text box. Justify Alignment: Aligns text evenly along both the left and right margins. Distributed Alignment: Distributes text evenly across the width, with equal spacing between characters and words.

questions.question: Symmetric encryption is also known as:
questions.explanation: conventional encryption. Public key encryption, also known as public key cryptography. The Hybrid Encryption primitive combines the efficiency of symmetric encryption with the convenience of public key (asymmetric) cryptography. Asymmetric encryption is a security technique that uses two different keys to encrypt and decrypt data.

questions.question: Which kind of computer security requires verifying the genuineness of the individuals or organisations that want to access a system?
questions.explanation: Authentication. Computer security (Cybersecurity), is the protection of computer systems and information from harm, theft, and unauthorized use. Q.1687. In MS Word, which feature allows you to create different headers and footers for odd and even pages in a document? SSC CHSL 02/07/2024 (1st shift) (c) Section Breaks Sol.1687.(a) Different Odd & Even Microsoft Word is a word processor program developed by Microsoft. It was first released on October 25, 1983, under the name Multi-Tool Word for Xenix systems. Initial release - October 25, 1983.

questions.question: On what type of data can MS Excel functions operate?

options.c: Range of cells or groups of cells

questions.explanation: Range of cells or groups of cells. MS Excel, a worksheet, also known as a spreadsheet, is a table into which you enter data and data label. Worksheet used in Excel documents is a collection of cells organized in rows and columns. It is the working surface you interact with to enter data. Each worksheet contains 1048576 rows and 16384 columns and serves as a giant table that allows you to organize information.

questions.question: What will happen when you select the cell contents of a particular row in MS-Excel and then click the Delete button?

options.a: First five cell entries will be deleted
options.b: Only first three cells will be deleted
options.d: Entries in all the selected cells will be deleted

questions.explanation: Microsoft Excel is a spreadsheet editor developed by Microsoft for Windows, macOS, Android, iOS and iPadOS. It features calculation or computation capabilities, graphing tools, pivot tables, and a macro programming language called Visual Basic for Applications (VBA). Excel forms part of the Microsoft 365 suite of software.

questions.question: Which of the following is the maximum file size that can be attached to an email message in Gmail?
questions.explanation: 25 MB. Email (electronic mail) is the exchange of computer-stored messages from one user to one or more recipients via the internet. Q.1691. In Microsoft PowerPoint, what is the shortcut key combination to insert a new slide in a presentation? SSC CHSL 02/07/2024 (4th shift) (a) Ctrl + I (b) Ctrl + N (c) Ctrl + D (d) Ctrl + M Sol.1691.(d) Ctrl + M. Microsoft PowerPoint is a presentation program, created by Robert Gaskins, Tom Rudkin and Dennis Austin at a software company named Forethought, Inc. Shortcut keys in Microsoft Powerpoint : Ctrl + I - Add or remove italics to selected text. Ctrl + N - Create a new slide. Ctrl + D - Duplicate the selected object or slide.

questions.question: In MS-Word, a number printed above the base line is called ___________ .
questions.explanation: superscript. In Microsoft Word, a superscript is a number or text that is printed above the baseline, smaller than the regular text. It is often used to represent exponents, footnotes, or endnotes. Examples: 10̣2 - 2 above 10 is called the superscript.

questions.question: The moderate margin of a page in MS Word can be applied to the page from which of the following?
questions.explanation: Page Layout Tab : This is where you adjust the layout settings of your page, including margins, orientation, and size.

questions.question: In Microsoft Excel, which chart type is best suited for comparing proportions or percentages of a whole?
questions.explanation: Pie Chart: A circular chart divided into sectors, each representing a proportion or percentage of the whole.

questions.question: Which of the following occupies less memory when saving an MS-Word file?
questions.explanation: docx file is a Microsoft Word document saved in the Open XML format. The .docx file extension is a combination of the words "doc" and "xml" and is an evolution of the older .doc format. Q.1696. Which was the first programmable general purpose computer in the world ? SSC CHSL 04/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) ENIAC (b) Mark I (c) Napier's bones (d) Pascaline Sol.1696.(a) ENIAC. Designed by John Mauchly and J. Presper Eckert. Mark I was designed in by Harvard graduate student Howard H. Aiken to solve advanced mathematical physics problems. Napier's bones is a manually - operated calculating device created by John Napier. Pascaline world's first calculator invented by mathematician Pascal. Q.1697. Which feature in MS Word allows you to see a document's layout and formatting as it will appear when printed? SSC CHSL 04/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) Screen View (b) Draft View (c) Print Preview (d) Reading View Sol.1697.(c) Print Preview. Draft View: Simplified view for editing, without layout details. Reading View: Optimized for on-screen reading, not editing. Q.1698. In a spreadsheet, page orientation is changed through ___________. SSC CHSL 04/07/2024 (3rd shift) (a) paper dialog box (b) print dialog box (c) page setup dialog box (d) format dialogue box Sol.1698.(c) page setup dialog box. This dialog box allows you to adjust various (landscape or portrait), Paper size, Margins, Headers and footers, Print area.

questions.question: Which of the following steps is used to format a cell in an MS Excel Spreadsheet?
questions.explanation: In Excel, cell alignment is how your text or numbers are positioned in the cell. You can align vertically, meaning towards the top, the middle or the bottom. And also align horizontally, meaning to the left, the center or to the right.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an alignment option for text in a cell ?
questions.explanation: Justified alignment is typically used in paragraph formatting to align text to both the left and right margins. Common shortcut keys related to line alignment in MS Word: Horizontal Alignment: Ctrl + L = Left align, Ctrl + E = Center align, Ctrl + R = Right align, Ctrl + J= Justify align (in some applications).

questions.question: How can we add text to a slide in MS-PowerPoint ?
questions.explanation: Textbox. In Microsoft PowerPoint, a Text Box is a graphical element that allows you to add text to a slide. It's a container that holds text, and you can position it anywhere on the slide.

questions.question: Rows in MS-Excel are identified by:
questions.explanation: Numbers. In MS Excel, rows are named after numbers like 1, 2, 3, 4 etc. and the columns are named with capital letters A, B, C etc.

questions.question: In Microsoft Word, which option allows you to exclude specific sections or types of text from the spelling and grammar check?
questions.explanation: Exclusion Dictionary. MS-WORD: It is an application component of MS-Office which is used to do document, brochure, invitation and book preparation. It enables us to prepare, modify, design and edit the desired documents. It was first released in 1983, under the name Multi-Tool Word for Xenix systems.

questions.question: Which MS Excel function can be used to sum up the numbers entered in a group of cells?
questions.explanation: SUM. Example of SUM function: =SUM(A1:A10) adds up the values in cells A1 through A10.

questions.question: The process of merging data from a data source into a main document in a word-processing application is called:
questions.explanation: Mail Merge. Data Accumulation: Refers to the process of collecting and storing data. Data Concatenation: Means linking or combining data from multiple sources into a single sequence. Data Integration: Involves combining data from multiple sources into a unified view.

questions.question: Which view in MS PowerPoint is useful for editing or formatting a single slide at a time?
questions.explanation: Normal view: Allows you to edit text and images, format slide elements, add animations and transitions, and focus on a single slide without distractions. Q.1707. Which of the following represents the Two - Factor Authentication (2FA)? SSC CHSL 05/07/2024 (3rd shift) (a) The use of two different credentials to verify identity (b) A tool for optimising system resources (c) A security measure to prevent physical damage to a PC (d) A technique for organising files and folders on a PC Sol.1707.(a) Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) is a security process that requires two different credentials to verify an individual's identity.

questions.question: What enables IoT devices to gather data and exchange it over the network?
questions.explanation: Actuators and sensors. The Internet of Things (IoT) refers to the interconnected network of physical devices, vehicles, appliances, and other objects embedded with sensors, software, and connectivity capabilities. Q.1709. Which of the following extensions is used to save MS-Power Point applications? SSC CHSL 05/07/2024 (4th shift) (a) .doc (b) .docx (c) .txt (d) .ppt Sol.1709.(d) .ppt. Microsoft PowerPoint is a presentation program that allows users to create slideshows and presentations using slides, images, videos, animations, and other features.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of system software ?
questions.explanation: Windows operating system. Some examples of operating systems include Apple macOS, Microsoft Windows, Google's Android OS, Linux OS, and Apple iOS.

questions.question: In MS Word, which option allows you to insert a new column in a table while simultaneously shifting the existing columns to the right ?
questions.explanation: Insert Column to the Right. In Microsoft Word: To add a column to the left of the cell, click Insert Left in the Rows and Columns group.

questions.question: When a new text document is created in MS-Office 2016, the default text size of the document is _____.
questions.explanation: 11 points. 11 point size means it is approximately 0.152778 inches. 72 points size means the text size equals 1 inch. The default font style in MS Word 2016 is Calibri. The default font and size can be changed in the Word Options or by selecting a different font or size for the document.

questions.question: Which of the following is an input device?
questions.explanation: Keyboard. Input devices provide data to the computer, while output devices receive data from the computer and present it to the user. Examples of Input devices : keyboards, computer mice, scanners, cameras, joysticks, and microphones, etc. Examples of Output devices : Monitor, Printers, Headphone, Computer Speaker, Projector.

questions.question: How can you access the Step by Step Mail Merge Wizard in Microsoft Word?
questions.explanation: Ctrl + M in Microsoft Word is used to indent a paragraph, not to start the Mail Merge Wizard. F12 opens the "Save As" dialog box in Microsoft Word, not developer tools (which happens in web browsers). Q.1715. In MS-Word, a document may be printed using ______________. SSC CHSL 08/07/2024 (3rd shift) (a) Ctrl+X (b) Ctrl+P (c) Ctrl+V (d) Ctrl+C Sol.1715.(b) Ctrl + P. Other important shortcut keys: Ctrl + X - Cuts selected text or files and copies them. Ctrl + V - Pastes the contents of your clipboard into the current cursor location on a PC. Ctrl + C - Copies highlighted text or images. Ctrl + N - Creates a new page. Ctrl + B - Bolds selected text. Ctrl + Z - Undoes a change made to a document. Ctrl + Y - Redoes an action. Q.1716. To create a new MS Word document, which of the following steps is correct? SSC CHSL 08/07/2024 (4th shift) (a) File Tab →Start (b) File Tab →Recent (c) File Tab →Open (d) File Tab →New Sol.1716.(d) File Tab →New. Some Microsoft Word keyboard shortcuts: Ctrl + O - Opens a document, Ctrl + W - Closes a document, Ctrl + B - Applies or removes bold formatting, Ctrl + I - Applies or removes italic formatting, Ctrl + U - Applies or removes underline formatting.

questions.question: Application software includes a variety of programs that are designed to meet the information processing needs of _____________.
questions.explanation: End users. Application software is a term used for software created for a specific purpose. Examples of application software include: Word Processing Software, Graphics Software, Spreadsheet Software, Presentation Software, Web Browsers.

questions.question: Where can you find the options ‘From Beginning’ and ‘From Current Slide’ to run a slide show in MS PowerPoint?
questions.explanation: Slide Show tab. Microsoft PowerPoint is a presentation program, created by Robert Gaskins and Dennis Austin. It was released on April 20, 1987.

questions.question: What should you click to choose the date for sorting in Gmail?
questions.explanation: The ‘Date within’ field. Gmail is the email service provided by Google. Created by - Paul Buchheit. Launched - April 1, 2004.

questions.question: Which keyboard key is used to jump the cursor to the next cell of the table in MS Word?
questions.explanation: Tab. In MS Word, pressing Tab moves the cursor to the next cell in the row, while pressing Shift+Tab moves the cursor to the previous cell.

questions.question: Which Excel feature allows you to simultaneously edit the same cell or range of cells with multiple users in real-time?
questions.explanation: Co-authoring. Microsoft Excel is a spreadsheet editor developed by Microsoft for Windows, macOS, Android, iOS and iPadOS. It features calculation or computation capabilities, graphing tools, pivot tables, and a macro programming language called Visual Basic for Applications (VBA). Excel forms part of the Microsoft 365 suite of software. Initial release date - September 30, 1985. Written in - C++ (back-end).

questions.question: Which key can be pressed to initiate a slide show in MS PowerPoint ?
questions.explanation: Function key F5. Some other function keys: F2 - Selects the text box that contains an object or text, F3 - When used with Shift, toggles between lower case, sentence case, and upper case for selected text. F7- Opens the Spelling dialog box to identify and correct spelling errors. F9 - When used with Shift, shows or hides gridlines.

questions.question: How does the CPU distinguish between each memory cell ?
questions.explanation: By its address. The Central Processing Unit (CPU) is the primary component of a computer that acts as its “control center.” It has three main parts: Arithmetic logic unit (ALU), control unit (CU), and memory unit.

questions.question: In PPT What method can be used to move among slides if your hands are already on the keyboard ?

options.c: Clicking on the slide thumbnail in the Slides tab
options.d: Pressing the Esc key to exit the presentation

questions.explanation: PowerPoint (PPT) is a presentation program that allows users to create, edit, and share multimedia presentations on their computer or mobile device.

questions.question: In MS Word, which icon from the icons below the Menu Bar is commonly used to save the current document?
questions.explanation: The floppy disk icon. It is on the upper-left corner of the Word app is the Save As button. It will open a file location tab where we can select and save our documents.

questions.question: In addition to using the arrow keys and mouse clicks, what other method can be used to move around a table in Microsoft Word?

options.a: Pressing the ‘Ctrl’ key and clicking on the cell
options.b: Pressing the ‘Enter’ key or using the scroll wheel
options.c: Using the Tab key or double-clicking on the cell
options.d: Right-clicking on the cell and selecting ‘Move to Next Cell’

questions.explanation: Some commonly used shortcuts in Microsoft Word for navigating the document: Ctrl + Left Arrow: Move the cursor one word to the left. Ctrl + Right Arrow: Move the cursor one word to the right. Ctrl + Up Arrow: Move the cursor up by one paragraph. Ctrl + Home: Move the cursor to the beginning of the document. Ctrl + End: Move the cursor to the end of the cursor to the top of the previous page. Q.1727. What function does the public key serve in digital signature verification? SSC CHSL 10/07/2024 (2nd shift) (a) To encrypt the digital signature (b) To decrypt the digital signature (c) To ensure confidentiality during transmission (d) To calculate the hash value of the message or file content Sol.1727.(b) In digital signature verification, the sender encrypts the hash of the message with their private key to create the digital signature.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of multi-factor authentication for PC security?
questions.explanation: Fingerprint scan. Multi-factor authentication (MFA) involves using two or more authentication methods to verify a user's identity.

questions.question: In MS Word, which option allows you to quickly remove all formatting from selected text while retaining the underlying text content ?
questions.explanation: Clear Formatting. Paste Special: It lets us paste content with source formatting, destination formatting, or as plain text. Format Painter: It is used to copy formatting from one section of text and apply it to another. It's helpful for maintaining consistency in document formatting.

questions.question: In Microsoft Office, how is the font size determined?
questions.explanation: Points. Microsoft Office was Initial release - October 1, 1990. It featured Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.

questions.question: The slide number can be added to each slide of the MS PowerPoint presentation from which of the following?
questions.explanation: Footer. Microsoft Power Point is a presentation program, created by Robert Gaskins, Tom Rudkin and Dennis Austin at a software company named Forethought, Inc. It was released on April 20, 1987, initially for Macintosh computers only. Operating system - Microsoft Windows. Written in - C++ (back-end).

questions.question: Formulas in MS-Excel always start with which of the following symbols?
In Microsoft Word, the Mail Merge Wizard is located under the _________ tab.
questions.explanation: Mailings. In MS Word, Mailings tab, manage mail merges with options to select recipients, insert fields, preview, and finish merges.

questions.question: Which of the following is the fastest way to open the Help window in Microsoft Word?

options.a: Click the Help button on the ribbon
options.b: Click the Microsoft Office button and then, click Help
options.c: Type ‘help’ in the search bar and then, press Enter

questions.explanation: Press F1. Function keys in Microsoft Word: F2 - Moves selected text or object to another location. F3 - Toggles the "AutoText" feature on/off. F4: Repeat last action. F5 - Go to a specific page, bookmark, or section (similar to Ctrl + G). F6 - Move cursor to task pane (e.g., Navigation, Styles). Q.1735. Which function is used to calculate the maximum value in a selected column in MS Excel? SSC CHSL 11/07/2024 (4th shift) (a) Auto max (b) Max (c) Auto high (d) High Sol.1735.(b) Max.

questions.question: What is ‘Phishing’ in the context of network security?

options.a: A type of computer worm that spreads through email attachments.
options.b: An attack that floods a network with excessive traffic to disrupt its operation.
options.c: A social engineering attack that tricks users into revealing sensitive information.
options.d: A type of malware that spreads through infected USB drives.

questions.explanation: Four types of phishing : Spear Phishing, Whaling, Vishing, Email Phishing. More types of cyber attacks: Malware (short for “malicious software”) is a file or code, typically delivered over a network, that infects, explores, steals or conducts virtually any behavior an attacker wants. Spoofing is the act of disguising a communication from an unknown source as being from a known, trusted source. Q.1737. What protocol is commonly used for sending and receiving emails? SSC CHSL Tier II 10/01/2024 (a) SSH (Secure Shell Protocol) (b) SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) (c) FTP (File Transfer Protocol) (d) HTTP (Hypertext Transfer Protocol) Sol.1737.(b) The Secure Shell (SSH) protocol is a method for securely sending commands to a computer over an unsecured network.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) in a Central Processing Unit (CPU)?

options.a: It executes program instructions by performing calculations and logical comparisons.
options.b: It manages the data flow between the CPU fan.
options.c: It controls the speed of the CPU fan.
options.d: It connects the CPU to peripheral devices.

questions.explanation: Logical operations: comparisons (greater than, less than, equal to), AND, OR, NOT. The primary function of an ALU is to store and retrieve data from memory. There can be more than one ALU in a CPU.

questions.question: Which keyboard shortcut opens File Explorer (previously known as Windows Explorer) in Windows 10?
questions.explanation: Win + E. File explorer shortcuts in Windows 11: Alt + D: Select the address bar. Ctrl + E: Select the search box. Ctrl + N: Open a new window. Num Lock + asterisk (*): Display all subfolders under the selected folder. Num Lock + plus (+) : Display the contents of the selected folder. Num Lock + minus (-): Collapse the selected folder.

questions.question: Which networking protocol is used to assign IP addresses dynamically to devices on a network?
questions.explanation: DHCP . It is a network protocol that is used to configure network devices to communicate on an IP network. An Internet Protocol (IP) address is the unique identifying number assigned to every device connected to the internet. IP (version 4) addresses are 32-bit integers that can be expressed in hexadecimal notation. An IPv6 address is 128 bits long compared with the 32 bit address of IPv4.

questions.question: In MS-Excel 365, you can use _________ when you want to change a cell reference within a formula without changing the formula itself.
questions.explanation: INDIRECT. Other functions: VLOOKUP - Looks for a value in the leftmost column of a table, and then returns a value in the same row from the column we specify. TRANSPOSE - Converts a vertical range of cells to a horizontal range, or vice versa. MATCH - Returns the relative position of an item in an array that matches a specified value in a specified order.

questions.question: In computer memory __________ bus is used to transfer addresses between CPU and the main memory.
questions.explanation: address bus. A computer bus is a set of conductors that enable information to be transmitted between computer components, such as printers, modems, and monitors.

questions.question: Which of the following parts of given URL is a 'domain name' ? http://www.india.in/tallybook/tallybook.x yz
questions.explanation: www.india.in. A URL (Uniform Resource Locator) is a unique identifier used to locate a resource on the Internet. http: This is the protocol used for communication, in this case, indicating Hypertext Transfer Protocol.

questions.question: Which of the following types of firewall operates at the Network layer to examine incoming and outgoing packets?
questions.explanation: packet filtering. It is a powerful security technique against intrusions from external networks. An application-level gateway (ALG) is a type of firewall that filters traffic at the application layer of the OSI (open systems interconnection) model. Q.1745. Which of the following statements is/are correct for email ? I. A user can check the email header prior to downloading. II. A user can partially download an email. SSC CHSL Tier II 02/11/2023 (a) Only II (b) Only I (c) Neither I nor II (d) Both I and II Sol.1745.(d) Both I and II. Different Parts of Email: Sender/from, subject line, pre-header, salutation, email body, Closing line, Signature, Call to Action (CTA), Attachments. A call to action (CTA), if defined, is an instruction to the reader that encourages them to take immediate action.

questions.question: In File Explorer of Windows 11, to reduce the space between files, select view > _________ view.
questions.explanation: Compact. In Windows 11 under View menu (Tiles, content, details pane, preview pane, show). Under Show menu (Navigation pane, compact view, item check boxes, file name extensions, hidden items).

questions.question: A _________ is a program that can self - replicate and propagate over the network.
questions.explanation: Worm. A boot sector virus is a type of malware that infects a system's boot partition or the Master Boot Record (MBR) of a hard disk. A macro virus is a computer virus written in the same macro language used to create software programs such as Microsoft Excel or Word.

questions.question: When the sender and receiver of an email are on the same system, we need only ______ user agents.
questions.explanation: two. When the sender and the receiver of an e-mail are on different systems, we need two UAs (User Agents) and a pair of MTAs (client and server). MTAs- Message transfer agents.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of RAM (Random Access Memory) in a computer?

options.c: Providing temporary storage for data that the CPU is currently processing

questions.explanation: RAM is a computer or laptop’s short-term memory. It’s where the data is stored that the computer processor needs to run applications and open files. The information stored in RAM can be checked with the help of BIOS (Basic Input / Output System). BIOS is the program a computer's microprocessor uses to start the computer system after it is powered on. RAM is volatile in nature, which means the data is lost when the device is switched off.

questions.question: What is a cell in Microsoft Excel?

options.a: A separate file used to store data.
options.b: A single worksheet within a workbook.
options.c: The intersection of a row and a column in a worksheet.

questions.explanation: Cells are the basic building blocks of a worksheet. A worksheet (also known as a spreadsheet) consists of cells in which we can enter and calculate data. The active cell is the selected cell in which data is entered when we begin typing. A range is a group of two or more cells in a worksheet. Q.1751. Which networking device operates at Layer 2 of the OSI model and forwards data based on MAC addresses? SSC CGL Tier II 26/10/2023 (a) Switch (b) Hub (c) Firewall (d) Router Sol.1751.(a) Switch. A network switch connects devices in a network to each other, enabling them to talk by exchanging data packets. A hub is a device that links multiple computers and devices together. A router is a device that connects two or more packet-switched networks.

questions.question: Which backup device provides a convenient way to create system images and complete backups, but may require a lengthy restoration process?
questions.explanation: A tape drive is a device that stores computer data on magnetic tape, especially for backup and archiving purposes. DVD Rewritable (DVD RW) discs are designed to store up to 4.7GB or 120 minutes of DVD-quality video.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an example of a web browser?
questions.explanation: Facebook (social networking site). Examples of social networking sites: Twitter, Instagram, Linkedin, Snapchat, Google meet. A web browser is an application for accessing websites and the Internet. Examples: Mozilla Firefox, Google Chrome, Opera, Safari, Microsoft Edge, Netscape Navigator, Mosaic.

questions.question: What is the function of the cache memory in a computer’s memory hierarchy?

options.a: To provide permanent storage for data and files.
options.b: To store frequently used data for faster access by the CPU.
options.c: To control the flow of data between the CPU and memory.
options.d: To manage the allocation of memory resources to different programs.

questions.explanation: Cache memory is a chip - based computer component that makes retrieving data from the computer's memory more efficient. It is an extremely fast memory type that acts as a buffer between RAM and the CPU. It holds frequently requested data and instructions so that they are immediately available to the CPU when needed. It is costlier than main memory or disk memory but more economical than CPU registers.

questions.question: What is a ‘Firewall’ in the context of network security?

options.a: A software application for managing files and folders.
options.b: A type of malware that spreads through email attachments.
options.c: A security mechanism that filters and controls network traffic.
options.d: A hardware device used to boost network performance.

questions.explanation: A firewall is defined as the system which analyzes and filters incoming or outgoing data packets based on predefined rules. Q.1756. In computer registers, MAR stands for ______. SSC CGL Tier II 26/10/2023 (a) Main Access Register (b) Memory Access Register (c) Memory Address Register (d) Main Address Register Sol.1756.(c) MAR (Memory Address Registers). It holds the address of the location to be accessed from memory. MAR and MDR (Memory Data Register) together facilitate the communication of the CPU and the main memory. Memory Data Registers: It contains data to be written into or to be read out from the addressed location. More types of registers : AC (accumulator), DR (Data registers), PC (Program counter), IR (index registers), MBR (Memory buffer registers).

questions.question: What is a ‘Trojan’ in the context of network security?

options.a: A protocol used for secure communication between devices on a network.
options.b: A program that monitors network traffic for security breaches.
options.c: A type of firewall that protects against viruses and malware.
options.d: Malicious software disguised as legitimate software to gain unauthorised access.

questions.explanation: A Trojan virus spreads through legitimate-looking emails and files attached to emails, which are spammed to reach the inboxes of as many people as possible.

questions.question: In File Explorer of Window 10, the keyboard shortcut ‘Num Lock + Asterisk sign (*)’ is used to ______.

options.a: display all subfolders under the selected folder
options.b: open the properties dialog box for the selected item
options.c: display the contents of the selected folder www.ssccgl

questions.explanation: File explorer keyboard shortcuts: Num Lock + plus (+): Display the contents of the selected folder. Num Lock + minus (-) : Collapse the selected folder. Ctrl + Shift + E: Display all folders above the selected folder. Ctrl + Shift + N: Create a new folder.

questions.question: In Windows 10, which of the following keyboard shortcuts is used to display and hide the desktop?
questions.explanation: Windows logo key + D. Windows logo key + G - It is used to open Xbox Game Bar when a game is open. Windows logo key + C - It is used to open chat from Microsoft Teams. Windows logo key + H - It is used to Launch voice typing.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a part of an email address?
questions.explanation: Photo. Parts of an email address are :- Username- The username is the name we choose to be identified with for e-mail purposes and that we have provided to the email host to create our email account. '@' symbol- @ (pronounced "at the rate of" ) is the symbol in an Email address that separates the name of the user from the user's Internet address. Mail Server - A mail server transfers and delivers email messages between two or more mail clients. Domain - A domain name forms part of the Uniform Resource Locator (URL) and is the address for a webpage. Example:- ssccglpinnacle@gmail.com.

questions.question: Identify whether the following statements are true or false with reference to MS-Word 2010. (i) A template can be selected while creating a new document. (ii) The keyboard shortcut for going to the end of a document is Shift + End.
questions.explanation: (i) True (ii) False. In Microsoft Word, templates are pre -designed documents. The template could be for a business card, brochure, resume, presentation.

questions.question: Which of the following types of hackers, also known as crackers, hack to gain unauthorised access to a system and harm its operations or steal sensitive information?
questions.explanation: Black Hat. A white hat (ethical hacker) is an individual who uses hacking skills to identify security vulnerabilities in hardware, software or networks. A red hat is a hacker who takes aggressive steps to stop black hat hackers. A gray hat is a computer security expert who sometimes violates ethical standards but doesn't have malicious intentions.

questions.question: Which of the following is a mini - DIN connector used to connect a keyboard and mouse to a PC compatible computer ?
questions.explanation: PS/2 port:- The PS/2 port is a 6-pin mini-DIN connector used for connecting keyboards and mice to a PC compatible computer system. It is also called a mouse port.

questions.question: What is the diameter of a Blu-ray Disc?
questions.explanation: 120 mm. Blu-ray Disc is 120 mm in diameter and 1.2 mm thick, the same size as DVDs (digital versatile/video disks) and CDs (Compact disks). Currently, 50GB and 25GB capacity, Blu-ray Disc media is available in both the BD-RE rewritable and BD-R recordable formats.

questions.question: The _______ unit is that part of the CPU which controls or directs the operations of the processor.
questions.explanation: Control. A control unit handles all processor control signals. The control unit is made up of flip-flops, logic gates, digital circuits and encoder and decoder circuits that are wired in a specific and fixed way.

questions.question: A ___________ is an electronic device that receives a weak signal and regenerates it.
questions.explanation: Repeater operates at the physical layer. It is a powerful network hardware device that regenerates an incoming signal from the sender before retransmitting it to the receiver.

questions.question: Which of the following shortcut keys is used to make the contents of a cell underlined in MS-Excel 365?
questions.explanation: Ctrl + 4. Some other shortcut key in MS-Excel 365: Ctrl + 0 - To hide the selected column, Ctrl + 1 - Open the Format Cells dialog box, Ctrl + 2 - Bold text or remove bold formatting, Ctrl + - Italicize text or remove italic formatting, Ctrl + 5 - Apply or remove strikethrough formatting, Ctrl+6 Hides and shows hidden objects, Ctrl + 8 - Display and hide the outline symbols, Ctrl + 9 -To hide a row.

questions.question: In a star network, how many lines are required for connecting N nodes to the host node?
questions.explanation: N-1. Network topology - Arrangement of a network, comprising nodes and connecting lines. There are 6 types of topology, Bus Topology - Every computer is connected to a common bus (single cable), Ring Topology - Every node is connected to two other nodes, forming a loop. Tree Topology - A central root node from which all other nodes branch out, Star Topology - Each device on the network is connected to a central hub, Mesh Topology ({n(n-1)/2})- Each node is connected to every other node in the network, Hybrid Topology - It is a combination of two or more network topologies.

questions.question: Which of the following shortcut keys is used to move the cursor one word to the left in MS-Word 365 ?
questions.explanation: Ctrl + Left arrow key. Ctrl + The Shift + left arrow and Shift + right arrow combinations - moving the cursor to the beginning and the end of a line respectively. Q.1770. Sam wants to share his presentation with larger audience via emails. Which feature of MS PowerPoint 365 can he use for it? SSC CHSL 10/03/2023 (4th Shift) (a) Save As (b) Print (c) Share (d) Info Sol.1770.(c) Share. Features of Microsoft Powerpoint 365 - Designer, Smart Lookup, QuickStarter, New charts, Morphing, Real-time collab, Version History, Zoom for non-sequential presentations. Q.1771. Natural language processing (NLP) is a branch of artificial intelligence that helps computers _______ human language. I. understand II. Interpret III. manipulate SSC CHSL 10/03/2023 (4th Shift) (a) I, II and III (b) only I and III (c) only I and II (d) only II and III Sol.1771.(a) I, II and III. Natural Language Processing (NLP) refers to the branch of Artificial Intelligence that gives the machines the ability to read, understand and derive meaning from human languages.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an application software ?
questions.explanation: Programming language translator software. Software is a set of instructions. Softwares are majorly classified into two categories: System Software and Application Software.

questions.question: Sheetal wants to create financial statements for her business. Which among the following software should she use for the same ?
questions.explanation: Spreadsheet software. It helps a user perform numerical functions and analyze numbers through a computerized version of an accounting worksheet. An Example - Excel.

questions.question: IBM 360 was developed in which of the following generations of computer ?
questions.explanation: Third generation. First Generation (1940 - 56): Vacuum tubes were used. Example: ENIAC (Electronic Numerical Integrator And Computer), EDVAC (Electronic Discrete Variable Automatic Computer), EDSAC (Electronic Delay Storage Automatic Calculator), UNIVAC I (Universal Automatic Computer I), IBM 701, IBM 650 etc.
